questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Enterprise Search?
options: To provide a dedicated organizational knowledge base that synthesizes information across company tools and data sources
To replace all regular chat conversations with a single unified interface
To automatically back up all company documents to Anthropic's servers
To monitor employee activity across all connected applications
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search is designed as a pre-built project for your entire organization, allowing you to find and synthesize knowledge buried across company tools and data sources. It is not a replacement for regular chats, does not involve backing up documents to external servers, and does not monitor employee activity.

questions.question: On which plan types is Enterprise Search available?
options: Free and Pro plans only
Team and Enterprise plans only
All plans including Free, Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise
Enterprise plans only
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search is available on Team and Enterprise plans only. It is not available on Free, Pro, or Max plans, and must be enabled by a workspace admin.

questions.question: What appears in the sidebar when Enterprise Search is enabled?
options: A generic 'Search' button that opens a new window
A dedicated 'Ask {Your Org Name}' option
Multiple separate project folders for each connected tool
An admin control panel for managing permissions
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search adds a dedicated 'Ask {Your Org Name}' option to the sidebar, where {Your Org Name} is customized by the admin during setup. This is a single unified interface, not multiple folders or an admin panel.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid use case for Enterprise Search?
options: Asking about company remote work policy and time-off procedures
Requesting real-time stock market data for investment decisions
Accessing personal email accounts of other employees
Generating new company policies without human review
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search is designed for questions about organizational policies, processes, and knowledge—such as remote work policies and time-off procedures. It does not provide real-time external data, does not grant access to others' personal accounts, and is not a policy-creation tool.

questions.question: What is the first step an admin must take to set up Enterprise Search for their organization?
options: Ask all employees to authenticate with their personal accounts
Click 'Ask Your Org' in the left sidebar and then click 'Set up for your org'
Connect all available tools before customizing the project name
Disable the feature temporarily to configure security settings
questions.explanation: The first step for admins is to click 'Ask Your Org' in the sidebar and then click 'Set up for your org' to begin the configuration process. User authentication happens after admin setup is complete, and tool connection comes after initiating setup.

questions.question: Which connectors are required during Enterprise Search admin setup?
options: Documents and Chat connectors; Email is optional
Chat and Email connectors; Documents is optional
All connectors must be enabled before setup can be completed
Only the Documents connector is required; all others are optional
questions.explanation: During admin setup, a Documents connector (like Google Drive or SharePoint) and a Chat connector (like Slack or Microsoft Teams) are required. Email is recommended but optional. Additional tools can be added later.

questions.question: How does Enterprise Search differ from a regular chat with connectors enabled?
options: Enterprise Search uses different AI models while regular chats use older models
Enterprise Search is specifically designed for information gathering with custom instructions configured by Anthropic, whereas regular chats with connectors are general-purpose
Enterprise Search requires manual setup of each conversation while regular chats are automatic
Enterprise Search can only access documents while regular chats can access all data types
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search is specifically designed for information gathering with custom instructions configured by the Anthropic team, making it distinct from general-purpose chats that happen to have connectors enabled. It is not about different AI models, setup complexity, or data type restrictions.

questions.question: What happens to user conversations and connected data in Enterprise Search?
options: Conversations are stored on Anthropic servers and data is indexed for faster searching
Conversations remain private and connected data is not indexed or stored separately
Both conversations and data are shared with all organization members for transparency
Data is automatically deleted after 30 days to comply with privacy regulations
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search maintains privacy by keeping conversations private and not indexing or storing connected data separately. This is a key security feature that distinguishes it from other data management approaches.

questions.question: What determines what information Enterprise Search can show you?
options: Only information that Anthropic has pre-approved for your organization
All information stored in connected tools, regardless of your individual permissions
Only information you already have permission to access in the original connected tools
Information based on your job title and department, as configured by admins
questions.explanation: Enterprise Search respects your existing permissions in the original tools. It only shows what you already have permission to access in those tools—it does not grant new access or bypass existing restrictions.

questions.question: An organization has connected SharePoint, Slack, Gmail, and Google Drive to Enterprise Search. A user wants to maximize the comprehensiveness of their search results. What should they do?
options: Authenticate with only the most frequently used tool to avoid overwhelming the system
Ask the admin to disable some connectors to improve search accuracy
Enable authentication with each service they want to search to maximize coverage
Wait for the admin to automatically sync all tools to their account
questions.explanation: The more connectors a user enables by authenticating with each service, the more comprehensive their search results will be. Users can add more connectors later through the project's Instructions section. Disabling connectors or waiting for automatic sync would reduce rather than maximize comprehensiveness.

questions.question: An admin is setting up Enterprise Search and needs to decide which tools to connect. The organization uses Slack for chat, Google Drive for documents, and Gmail for email. Based on the setup requirements, which configuration would be valid?
options: Connect only Slack and Google Drive, skip Email since it is optional
Connect Slack, Google Drive, and Gmail, then add more tools later if needed
Connect only Google Drive and Gmail, since Slack is not a required connector
Connect all available tools simultaneously before customizing the project name
questions.explanation: The setup requires a Documents connector (Google Drive) and a Chat connector (Slack). Email (Gmail) is recommended but optional. This configuration meets the requirements and allows for adding more tools later. The other options either skip required connectors or misunderstand the requirement structure.

questions.question: A new employee joins an organization that has Enterprise Search enabled. They see 'Ask CompanyName' in their sidebar but receive limited search results when asking questions. What is the most likely reason?
options: The admin has not finished the initial setup process for Enterprise Search
The employee has not yet authenticated with the services they want to search
The organization's data sources are too large for Enterprise Search to index
The employee's account does not have the correct plan type for Enterprise Search
questions.explanation: If the project appears in the sidebar, admin setup is complete. Limited results indicate the user has not authenticated with all relevant services. The user setup process requires individual authentication with each service (Slack, Google, Microsoft 365, etc.) to enable searching those sources. Without authentication, those services cannot be searched.
